mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in real retail sales signals stronger household consumption, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 5-day forward returns following positive RSAFS changes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, restrictive monetary policy raises real discount rates and borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative signal instances produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered months during any 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in industrial production and increase in consumer prices signals stagflationary supply contraction, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month while CPIAUCSL increases month-over-month
failure_condition: Instances of the joint trigger do not produce negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of occurrences over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is below 4.0%, a three-month increase in UNRATE predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because the labor market shifts from full employment to early deterioration, reducing corporate earnings visibility.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences during any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained three-month increase in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns following 3-month increases in INDPRO do not significantly exceed zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a federal funds rate above 5% and a rising unemployment rate over the prior three months produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because restrictive monetary policy coincides with labor market deterioration, amplifying recession risk.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with emerging labor weakness
failure_condition: Triggered joint-signal instances fail to produce negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of occurrences over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in both retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based demand destruction across consumer and residential investment sectors, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month and HOUST declines month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST do not precede negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A negative month-over-month change in nonfarm payrolls signals contraction in aggregate labor income and impending consumer spending retrenchment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in PAYEMS are not followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.
